body: import java.util.List;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import org.junit.Test;
import org.apache.commons.validator.routines.DomainValidator.ArrayType;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;
import org.bitstrings.test.junit.runner.ClassLoaderPerTestRunner;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;

translation: # Get an instance of the DomainValidator
# Assertions for the first validator instance
# Create a list of items for the second validator instance
# Get another instance of the DomainValidator with the items
# Assertions for the second validator instance
self.assertTrue(validator.isValidGenericTld("gp"), "Expected 'gp' to be a valid generic TLD")
self.assertFalse(validator.isValidGenericTld("com"), "Expected 'com' to be an invalid generic TLD")
self.assertTrue(validator.isValidCountryCodeTld("cp"), "Expected 'cp' to be a valid country code TLD")
self.assertFalse(validator.isValidCountryCodeTld("ch"), "Expected 'ch' to be an invalid country code TLD")
# Update TLD override with ArrayType.GENERIC_PLUS and a new TLD
# Assert that the instance is not None
# Attempt to update TLD override again and expect an RuntimeError
with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="Can only invoke this method before calling getInstance"):
